commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR defers gateway provider auth-state prewarm to a short post-ready timer, passes current runtime config into delayed warmup, and adds startup regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. for the introduced patch issue: the current GitHub check annotation shows extra `warm-provider-auth` events in `server-reload-handlers.test.ts`, and source inspection shows the new timer has no lifecycle stop handle. I did not rerun the original vLLM latency repro, but the PR supplies after-fix live gateway/vLLM output for the ordering slice.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can make the delayed provider-auth prewarm lifecycle-cancelable and fix the failing gateway reload check; maintainer acceptance of the auth timing tradeoff still remains before merge.

target.title: fix(gateway): defer provider auth prewarm after startup
target.body:
## Summary

Gateway `sessions_spawn` could be slow right after startup because provider auth-state prewarm started as soon as sidecars became ready.
That prewarm is useful for later model-listing calls, but it is not required for accepting gateway tool requests.
This change moves provider auth-state prewarm onto a short post-ready timer so early `/tools/invoke` requests get a chance to run first, while preserving the auth-failure rewarm hook.

Fixes part of #63229.

## What Changed

Provider auth prewarm now runs as a scheduled post-ready task instead of inline on the sidecars-ready promise.
Tests can disable that background task unless a specific test enables it, so startup tests do not leak delayed timers.

- Added `scheduleProviderAuthStatePrewarm` for delayed provider auth warmup and rewarm hook registration.
- Kept auth-profile failure handling by clearing and rewarming the prepared provider auth-state map.
- Passed current runtime config into delayed prewarm so an early reload cannot be overwritten by stale startup config.
- Added regression coverage proving post-ready gateway work runs before delayed provider auth prewarm starts.

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts` - passed, 39 tests.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/tools-invoke-http.test.ts` - passed, 132 tests across 4 files.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts` - passed, 124 tests across 5 files.
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_SHARD_NAME=agentic-control-plane-startup-runtime OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=<20-path include file> node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts` - passed, 219 tests across 20 files.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server.impl.ts` - passed.
- `git diff --check` - passed.
- `codex review --base upstream/main` - passed after fixing the stale-config review finding; reran clean on the current head.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: early gateway `/tools/invoke` `sessions_spawn` acceptance should not wait behind provider auth-state prewarm during startup.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw gateway on this branch, auth disabled on loopback, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, local vLLM serving `Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct` as `qwen-a` on an OpenAI-compatible loopback endpoint, and gateway default model set to `vllm/qwen-a`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: started vLLM with `vllm serve Qwen/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000 --served-model-name qwen-a --max-model-len 2048 --gpu-memory-utilization 0.2`; started the patched gateway with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --bind loopback --auth none --port 18890 --allow-unconfigured --verbose`; polled `/health`; immediately posted to `/tools/invoke` with `tool=sessions_spawn`, `sessionKey=main`, `agentId=main`, and `runTimeoutSeconds=5`.

Evidence after fix: redacted runtime log excerpt and copied live output from the local gateway/vLLM run:

```text
/v1/models returned qwen-a from the local vLLM server.
Gateway log: 2026-05-22T16:09:10.290+08:00 [gateway] agent model: vllm/qwen-a (thinking=medium, fast=off)
Gateway log: 2026-05-22T16:09:10.984+08:00 [gateway] ready
Client:      2026-05-22T16:09:18+08:00 POST /tools/invoke sessions_spawn
Response:    HTTP_STATUS=200
Response:    sessions_spawn_http_seconds=0.44 exit=0
Response:    details.status="accepted", modelApplied=true
Gateway log: 2026-05-22T16:09:48.869+08:00 [gateway] provider auth state pre-warmed in 36875ms
```

Observed result after fix: live output showed `sessions_spawn` accepted in 0.44 seconds, about 30 seconds before provider auth-state prewarm completed; the accepted response included a child session key, run id, `status: "accepted"`, and `modelApplied: true`.

What was not tested: I did not rerun the original large-model repro; this PR only proves the startup/prewarm ordering problem and does not claim to fix every symptom in #63229, including any separate healthy-vLLM false-timeout path.

## Risks

The change delays an optimization, not a required readiness step.
The main risk is that model-listing calls immediately after gateway ready may miss the prepared auth map for about one second and use the existing fallback path.

- Runtime provider auth-state prewarm still runs after the delay.
- Auth-profile failures still clear and schedule a rewarm.
- This does not claim to fix every symptom in #63229.
target.author: dutifulbob
